title: Settings
subtitle: Manage language, workspace, basics, and extensions.
clawEnabledDesc: When enabled, Claw runs scheduled tasks, the Feishu / Lark bridge, and the local IM webhook. Turning it off stops those background features, but does not affect normal GUI chats.
clawDefaultWorkspaceDesc: Leave this empty to use the GUI default working directory. Individual IM connections can still override it.
clawSkillAllowlistDesc: One Skill name per line. Claw prepends these as a policy hint before IM and scheduled prompts.
clawExtraSkillDirsDesc: Optional local Skill directories Claw should mention to the core agent.
clawPromptPrefixDesc: Prepended to IM webhook and scheduled task prompts.
clawPromptPrefixPlaceholder: Example: You are my personal automation assistant. Identify the source before answering.
clawRunModeDesc: Default mode for webhook turns and scheduled tasks.
clawModelDesc: Claw supports auto, pro, and flash model routing.
clawWorkspaceOverrideDesc: Leave empty to use the default working directory.
clawTasksDesc: Each task sends its prompt to DeepSeek-TUI on the configured schedule.
clawFeishuDesc: Configure bot credentials for scheduled delivery to Feishu and optional phone chat via the long-connection bridge.
clawFeishuStep1: Create an enterprise app on the Feishu Open Platform and enable bot + message send/receive permissions.
clawFeishuStep2: Enter App ID / App Secret below and save (writes [automation.feishu] in config.toml).
clawFeishuStep3: Message the bot in Feishu, copy your open_id from pairing hints or bridge logs, and paste it as Receive ID.
clawFeishuStep4: Use Send test to verify delivery; for mobile Agent chat run scripts/start-feishu-bridge.sh.
clawFeishuReceiveIdDesc: Saved as feishu_chat_id in config.toml; used for scheduled delivery and test messages.
clawFeishuWebhookSecretDesc: If set, export DEEPSEEK_FEISHU_WEBHOOK_SECRET when starting the runtime (must match bridge/inbound requests).
clawFeishuBridgeHint: Uses official SDK long connection; no public URL required. Set DEEPSEEK_ALLOW_UNLISTED=true for first pairing.
clawTasksNeedRuntime: Connect the local runtime (deepseek serve --http) before managing scheduled tasks.
clawTasksEmptyHint: In chat, click Automation next to the model picker and describe a schedule, e.g. email me Xiaomi stock price every day at 10:00.
clawTaskDeleteConfirm: Delete scheduled task “{{name}}”? This cannot be undone.
clawTaskPromptPlaceholder: Example: Summarize recent workspace changes and list follow-ups.
themeDesc: Light, dark, or match the operating system.
onboardingPreviewDesc: Reopen the first-launch setup dialog from Settings to debug its layout and system-theme behavior.
fontScaleDesc: Adjust the overall UI text size.
petMascotEnabledDesc: Show a Petdex companion above the composer; choose the active pet here.
petMascotPickDesc: Changes sync to the chat composer immediately.
turnCompleteNotificationDesc: Show a native Windows/macOS notification when an agent reply finishes.
deepseekBinaryHint: Leave empty to use the bundled installer; the official binary is fetched on first connect when needed.
tuiUpdateDesc: When the GUI manages the runtime, it can check npm for new deepseek-tui releases and upgrade in one click.
tuiUpdateUnknown: Updates have not been checked yet
tuiUpdateInstallingDesc: Downloading the npm package and preparing the new local runtime.
tuiUpdateRestarted: The managed runtime restarted with the new version.
tuiUpdateRestartPending: The upgrade finished; the runtime will use it next time it starts.
tuiUpdateNextLaunch: The upgrade finished and will apply on the next runtime start.
portDesc: Port used by the managed `deepseek serve --http` runtime.
runtimeTokenDesc: Auto-managed by Workbench (cached at ~/.deepseek/runtime.token, mode 0600). Click Regenerate to rotate.
configFilePathDesc: DeepSeek auth and Base URL are stored in this file.
skillsLocationDesc: Choose the default Skill directory used by Plugins > Skill.
skillsPathDesc: Directory-level options only; after installing from the market, place Skills in the directory above.
skillsScanDirsDesc: One local path per line. These extra Skill directories are passed along as hints for Claw tasks and IM turns.
skillsActionsDesc: Pick Skills on the ModelScope market and add them to the directory above.
skillsMarketplaceHint: Opens the ModelScope Skills market in your browser. Install chosen Skills into your local directory.
skillsRootUnavailable: This directory is unavailable. Choose a workspace-backed option or switch to a global Skill directory.
mcpPathDesc: This is the config file DeepSeek TUI reads from.
mcpEditorDesc: Edit the MCP registry (JSON, usually with an mcpServers object). API keys and Base URL stay in config.toml under Base.
mcpMarketplaceJsonHint: Find an MCP service on the ModelScope market, paste its JSON snippet here (usually under mcpServers), then save and restart the runtime.
hooksDesc: Hooks are configured in the [hooks] section of config.toml and can run scripts or forward events around tool calls.
hooksConfigPathDesc: Add lifecycle hooks with [[hooks.hooks]] in config.toml; list events via the TUI command /hooks events.
hooksActionsDesc: Restart the local runtime after changes for them to fully apply.
hooksOpenConfigHint: Hook scripts default to ~/.deepseek/hooks; lifecycle entries live in [hooks] inside ~/.deepseek/config.toml.
mcpFileStatusMissing: The config file does not exist yet. It will be created on first save.
mcpRuntimeHint: Reload refreshes the list; when the runtime is online it also tries to reconnect MCP. Restart the runtime if changes still do not apply after saving.
mcpReloadRuntimeOk: MCP config re-read from disk and runtime asked to reconnect servers.
mcpReloadDiskOnly: MCP config re-read from disk. Runtime is offline, so MCP was not reconnected; changes apply when the runtime starts.
mcpListDesc: When off, the runtime will not load tools from this server; the entry stays in your local config.
mcpListEmpty: No MCP servers yet. Use Install from market on ModelScope, then paste JSON in Advanced below.
pluginsInstalledDesc: Scans ~/.deepseek/skills for Skills; installed plugins are enabled by default.
pluginsListDesc: Skills in the directory below are enabled by default and loaded at runtime.
pluginsListEmpty: No Skills in this directory yet. Use Install from market on ModelScope and add them here.
pluginsRuntimeHint: Refresh list only rescans ~/.deepseek/skills; it does not hot-reload the runtime. Put new Skills downloaded from the market in the directory above.
apiKeyDesc: Required. Synced into the TUI config file and applied after the runtime reloads.
baseUrlDesc: Custom model service endpoint. Saved to the TUI config file; leave empty to restore the default endpoint.
firstRunTitle: Set up the essentials before you start chatting.
firstRunSubtitle: Choose your language, add the DeepSeek API key, and optionally point the app at a custom compatible endpoint.
firstRunChangeLater: You can change these values anytime from Settings after the workbench opens.
firstRunPreviewHint: This reuses the first-launch flow; unsaved theme previews are restored when you close it.
firstRunBuyApiHint: Don't have an API key yet? Visit DeepSeek's official platform to buy official API access, then come back here to paste it in.
firstRunApiKeyValidation: Please enter a DeepSeek API key before continuing.
autoStartDesc: Recommended. Let the GUI launch and manage the local runtime automatically when needed.
corsOriginsDesc: Additional allowed Origin values for the local HTTP server.
approvalPolicyDesc: Controls when the agent must ask before running tools (edit_file, apply_patch, shell). Saved values are synced into the TUI config and applied after the runtime reloads.
sandboxModeDesc: What the agent is allowed to touch on disk. Saved values are synced into the TUI config and applied after the runtime reloads.
autoApplyBlocked: Fix the port to apply changes
workspaceRootDesc: First launch uses ~/.deepseekgui/default_workspace by default, and you can change it to another directory.
apiKeyRequiredBody: Add your DeepSeek API key here first. Once entered, the GUI will download the bundled TUI if needed and start `deepseek serve --http` for you.
guiUpdateChannelDesc: Frontier receives new builds first; stable only receives formal stable releases.
guiUpdateDesc: Check the selected release channel for GUI updates, then download and install in the app.
guiUpdateCheckFailed: Could not check for GUI updates
guiUpdateErrNotConfigured: Update checking isn't available right now.
guiUpdateErrUnsupported: This build does not support in-app automatic updates. Open the download page to install manually.
guiUpdateErrRepoNotFound: The update source «{{repo}}» wasn't found or isn't reachable on GitHub.
guiUpdateErrForbidden: Couldn't fetch update information. Try again later.
guiUpdateErrRateLimit: Too many requests. Try again later.
guiUpdateErrNoStableVersion: No stable release or tag to compare yet (for example v0.1.0). Repo: {{repo}}.
guiUpdateAvailableManual: New version available: {{current}} → {{latest}}. Install it from the download page.
guiUpdateDownloadedDesc: Restart the app to install the new version.
logDirDesc: Error logs are written to daily files in this folder.
logEnabledDesc: Write tool call errors, request failures, and runtime errors to a local log file. Logs are cleaned up automatically.
logRetentionDesc: Delete log files older than the selected number of days.
preloadBridgeError: The app bridge is not available. Restart the application or check the preload path.
